## **10.1** 

## **NPC and Monster Action Announcement** 

The game master must manage the actions of NPCs and monsters. In combat, the planned actions of NPCs and monsters must be announced at the beginning of the round. 

At this time, the game master does not need to make a specific announcement. All they have to do is tell you what you can understand from the character's perspective. 

However, that does _not_ mean that you can make loose announcements and change them depending on the situation. 

For example, it’s correct to announce the planned action of a hellhound with the ability to breathe fire by saying, _I'm about to do something with my mouth wide open…_ The characters have no way of knowing if the hellhound is about to bite them or breathe fire. However, the game master should decide in advance which actions they are going to take. 

They must _not_ change things based on the circumstances, such as attacking with fangs if the character used the spell _Counter Magic_ to increase their resistance, or breathing fire if the character used the spell _Quickness_ to raise their agility (evasion speed). This can lead to a loss of game fairness and may also spoil player motivation. 

Strictly plan your actions, and when telling the players, _only_ tell them things that only the _character_ knows. 

## **10.2** 

## **Combat in Special Situations** 

Combat often occurs in unusual locations, such as in the dark, on poor footing, etc. In such cases, a penalty (sometimes a bonus) is given to an adventurer's attack power or evasion speed, depending on the situation. If the same conditions apply to monsters, the same penalty (bonus) will be given to the monster's attack and evasion points. 

This penalty will be considered in accordance with 9.3: General Modifiers (see p. 136). 

**Table 10-1: Attack Power and Evasion Speed Modifiers** 

|**Table 10-1: Attack Power and Evasion Speed Modifers**||
|---|---|
|Full movement (Evasion speed only)|-4|
|Can’t see opponent (in the dark, invisible enemy, etc.)<br> However, if alternative senses (smell, hearing, etc.) are developed to some extent<br> If you don't have to rely on vision|<br>-4<br>-2<br>None|
|When footing is bad<br> (Rocks and debris are everywhere, water is up to your waist, etc.)|-2|
|Fallen<br> (Same as when trying to stand up because you're in an unstable state)|-2|



An example is shown in Table 10-1: Attack Power and Evasion Speed Modifiers. 

## **10.3** 

## **Surprise Attacks** 

When encountering a monster, one of you may notice the other without them noticing you, allowing you to perform a **surprise attack** . This should also be determined by the game master depending on the situation, but we've included Table 10-2: Surprise Attack Check as a reference. The game master must roll 2D and add it to the monster level (the highest one in the case of a group). Next, all adventurers make a success roll using _ranger skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score. Subtract the monster's score from the 

highest final score. On the _Surprise Attack Check_ table, if this score is lower than the one in the _monster_ column, the monster has a chance for a surprise 

attack, and if it's greater than the one in the _adventurer_ column, the adventurers have a chance for a surprise attack. However, you should also take into 

**Table 10-2: Surprise Attack Check** 

|**Table 10-2: Surprise Attack Check**|||
|---|---|---|
|Intelligence|Monster|Adventurer|
|None, almost none, obeys commands|-6|+6|
|Animal, low|-5|+7|
|Human|-4|+8|
|High|-3|+9|

one round. The side that was surprised is unable to take any assertive action. It’s still possible to take actions such as trying to avoid enemy attacks or resisting magic cast on you. However, evading an 

consideration conditions such as the surrounding environment and whether the adventurers are proceeding quietly. 

If a surprise attack occurs, the side who surprised the other is free to act for 

## **Water Combat** 

## **10.4** 

Depending on the situation, adventurers may have to fight in water. 

spellsongs will be unusable. However, if you cast the spirit magic _Water Breathing_ beforehand, you can speak normally and cast spells. 

If the water is chest-high, your freedom of movement is restricted and you suffer a -2 penalty on hit and evasion checks. There are no restrictions on the weapons or magic you can use. 

Also, if you cast the spell _Decrease Water Pressure_ , which is also spirit magic, you can fight without suffering any of these penalties. At this time, you can use any weapon except projectiles. However, projectiles _cannot_ be used. 

If you are head-deep in water, you suffer a -4 penalty on hit and evasion checks. Additionally, the only weapons you can use are daggers, short swords, one-handed spears, or something similar. Other weapons and shields _cannot_ be used. Also, since you cannot make sound normally underwater, _all_ spells and 

As explained in the _swim_ section under Common Adventurer Abilities, you can only hold your breath underwater for a number of rounds equal to your life force (points). Unless 

## **Attacks Against the Sleeping** 

## **10.5** 

_Silence_ or _Mute_ magic, there will be no sound, so there is no need for a success roll. 

## **Rules for Sleeping Monsters** 

To get within 5 meters of a sleeping monster, you must successfully perform the thief skill _stealthy step_ . If you succeed on a success roll using _thief skill level + dexterity bonus_ as the baseline score, the monster will not wake up. If you fail, the monster will sense your presence and wake up immediately. 

Attacks against sleeping monsters are _always_ a success unless you roll 2D and get _double ones_ on your hit check (even when attacking a sleeping opponent, double ones is always a failure). Furthermore, when making a damage check, you can reduce the weapon's critical target by -1. If you are attacking with the _thief_ skill, your critical target will be -2. 

The game master must set the monster's monster level as the difficulty and add a 2D roll to it to determine the target score. Depending on the situation (for example, there are pebbles on the floor and they make a lot of noise, or the monster happens to be wearing earplugs), you can apply positive or negative modifiers to the final score (at this time, the reason you must use a difficulty instead of a target score is because there is a possibility that the monster will continue to pretend to be asleep even though it's aware). If you use 

If you attack a sleeping monster but do not deal any damage, or if you deal damage but are unable to reduce its life points to 0, the monster will wake up immediately. It will probably become angry and counterattack the adventurer starting on the next round. 

If there is combat, etc. going on in the surrounding area or a loud noise is made, the monster will wake up immediately. 

attack will incur a -4 penalty to evasion speed (points). 

you cast _Water Breathing_ , you will drown if you do not reach the surface within this number of rounds. 

If the opponent you're fighting is a creature that lives on land, such as a human, they will suffer the same penalty. However, creatures that live underwater, such as mermen, do not suffer any penalties other than weapon restrictions. Conversely, these creatures become slower on land and typically suffer a -4 penalty to hit and evasion. Some creatures, such as hydra and scylla, can act both in water and on land without penalty. 

In the case of something sleeping due to the ancient magic _Sleep Cloud_ or the spellsong _Lullaby_ , a resistance check must be made every round to judge. If it's natural sleep, there is no need to roll and it'll always wake up. 

However, the spirit magic _Sleep_ is an exception. Unless the magic itself is broken by _Dispel Magic_ or other magic belonging to _mind_ , it will never wake up. 

## **Rules for Sleeping Adventurers** 

If a sleeping adventurer is approached by a monster, the baseline score is _adventurer level + intelligence bonus_ , and if you succeed on a resistance roll against target score 9, you can wake up before being attacked. If the monster has the ability to sneak up on you, the target score will be even higher. If they approach using _stealthy step_ , etc. their final score will become the target score.

_Silence_ or _Mute_ magic, there will be no sound, so there is no need for a success roll. 

## **Rules for Sleeping Monsters** 

To get within 5 meters of a sleeping monster, you must successfully perform the thief skill _stealthy step_ . If you succeed on a success roll using _thief skill level + dexterity bonus_ as the baseline score, the monster will not wake up. If you fail, the monster will sense your presence and wake up immediately. 

Attacks against sleeping monsters are _always_ a success unless you roll 2D and get _double ones_ on your hit check (even when attacking a sleeping opponent, double ones is always a failure). Furthermore, when making a damage check, you can reduce the weapon's critical target by -1. If you are attacking with the _thief_ skill, your critical target will be -2. 

The game master must set the monster's monster level as the difficulty and add a 2D roll to it to determine the target score. Depending on the situation (for example, there are pebbles on the floor and they make a lot of noise, or the monster happens to be wearing earplugs), you can apply positive or negative modifiers to the final score (at this time, the reason you must use a difficulty instead of a target score is because there is a possibility that the monster will continue to pretend to be asleep even though it's aware). If you use 

If you attack a sleeping monster but do not deal any damage, or if you deal damage but are unable to reduce its life points to 0, the monster will wake up immediately. It will probably become angry and counterattack the adventurer starting on the next round. 

If there is combat, etc. going on in the surrounding area or a loud noise is made, the monster will wake up immediately.

If the opponent you're fighting is a creature that lives on land, such as a human, they will suffer the same penalty. However, creatures that live underwater, such as mermen, do not suffer any penalties other than weapon restrictions. Conversely, these creatures become slower on land and typically suffer a -4 penalty to hit and evasion. Some creatures, such as hydra and scylla, can act both in water and on land without penalty. 

In the case of something sleeping due to the ancient magic _Sleep Cloud_ or the spellsong _Lullaby_ , a resistance check must be made every round to judge. If it's natural sleep, there is no need to roll and it'll always wake up. 

However, the spirit magic _Sleep_ is an exception. Unless the magic itself is broken by _Dispel Magic_ or other magic belonging to _mind_ , it will never wake up. 

## **Rules for Sleeping Adventurers** 

If a sleeping adventurer is approached by a monster, the baseline score is _adventurer level + intelligence bonus_ , and if you succeed on a resistance roll against target score 9, you can wake up before being attacked. If the monster has the ability to sneak up on you, the target score will be even higher. If they approach using _stealthy step_ , etc. their final score will become the target score. 

139

If you're put to sleep by magical means such as _Sleep Cloud_ , you must make a check via mental power resistance roll each round as long as combat continues. 

When a monster, etc. attacks a sleeping adventurer, the attack will automatically hit. If the attack does not reduce life to 0, the adventurer wakes up immediately. Therefore, the quickest way to wake up your friends is to kick them. 

The spirit magic _Sleep_ is still an exception. Unless the magic itself is broken by _Dispel Magic_ or other magic belonging to _mind_ , they'll never wake up. 

## **10.6** 

## **Combat Between Monsters** 

Sometimes there will have to be combat between monsters. In that case, do the following: 

## 《 **Hit Checks** 》 

Add a 2D roll to the attacking monster's attack points - 7 (marked in brackets). If it’s equal to or greater than the evasion points of the defending monster, it's a hit. 

## 《 **Damage** 》 

If the attack hits, roll 2D. If it's double ones, the damage will be equal to the strike points of the attacking monster. Otherwise, the damage equals the attacking monster's strike points minus the defending monster's defense points. If the difference is 0 or less, only 1 point of damage will be dealt. 


![](harness/cache/e5870dca757ef182/images/Sword_World_RPG_-_Complete_Edition.pdf-0140-10.jpg)

## **Chapter 11: Notes on Magic** 

141

## **Rune Masters’ Status** 

## **11.1** 

In Alecrast, there aren't that many beings called rune masters. Rune masters are respected in a sense by the general public because they have special powers called magic. However, public sentiment towards magic is far from good. This is because, in the days of the ancient kingdom, citizenship was not recognized for anyone _except_ rune 

masters, and all other people were used as slaves. Therefore, there is a great sense of crisis regarding the power of rune masters (especially sorcerers). Some believe that once their numbers become sufficient and they regain the power they had during the ancient kingdom, the age of darkness may come again. 

Although rune masters are not openly persecuted due to the necessity of 

## **Dark Magic** 

## **11.2** 

As mentioned earlier, these runes, like holy magic, use the gods as its source of power. However, it is derived from the power of the gods of evil (wicked gods) such as Phalaris, who are believed to cause harm to us humans. For this reason, the rune masters of these runes are both hated and feared by the general public. A rune master who uses **dark magic** is called a **dark priest** . In a narrow sense, only the priests of Phalaris are sometimes called dark priests. This is because believers in wicked gods other than Phalaris are rarely seen. 

## 《 **Divination** 》 

Like the priest skill, this is the ability to pray and communicate with a god. 

## 《 **Vow** 》 

Similar to the priest skill, this is the ability to make a voluntary vow and have a god protect you. Wicked gods often impose harsh punishments on actions that violate their vows. 

## **11.2.2** 

Dark magic basically has the same origin as holy magic, and dark priests can use most of the basic holy magic. In addition, dark priests can use wicked magic that the gods of light do not allow (do not give power to). Furthermore, just as there is special holy magic that is unique to each god among holy magic, there is also special dark magic. 

## **Basic Dark Magic List** 

Here is a list of common dark magic that any priest of the wicked gods can use. In addition to these, priests of the wicked gods can _also_ use most of the basic holy magic. However, if an identical _type_ of spell or a spell with an identical _name_ is listed under dark magic, they can only use _that_ version of it. 

## **11.2.1** 

## **1st Level Basic Dark** 

## **Basic Abilities of the Dark Priest Skill** 

## **Magic List** 

## **[ Wounds ]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=5 

Dark priests can use the following abilities: 

Distance=10 meters Area=One 

Duration=Instant 

Effect=Cut up the opponent's body, dealing strike power 10 damage Type=Damage (Special) 

their presence, they are also viewed with fear and disgust by the general public. Countries that are negative towards magic sometimes have policies that exile rune masters from the country. 

Of course, these people also rely on magical power when they themselves need it. 

## Expansion=Final score, distance, targets, damage certainty Resist=Negated effect 

This spell has the opposite effect of _Cure Wounds_ , cutting into the target's body and causing wounds. This damage check is made with strike power 10 (criticals can also occur). No damage is dealt to a target that attempts and successfully resists. 

This spell also has no effect on anything that does not have normal life, such as undead or magical creatures. 

## **[ Mental Attack ]** 

Base Mental Power Cost=1~ 

Distance=Touch Area=One 

Duration=Instant 

Effect=Reduces the target's mental power (points) by double the mental power consumed by the caster. 

Type=- 

Expansion=Final score, effect (see description) Resist=Negated effect 

This is a very special spell. Before using this spell, you must declare in advance how many points of mental power (points) you will use. You can reduce the target's mental power (points) by _twice_ the mental power (points) consumed. The caster can use any number of points up to the amount of mental power they have.
